caption:
Stop using Object.freeze() for immutable data.

Record and Tuple are deeply immutable by default. They compare by VALUE, not reference. And they work as Map keys.

ES2026 proposal. Which pattern do you prefer? A or B?

#javascript #immutable #es2026 #webdev #coding #programming #developer #cleancode #functional #codingtips

a.label: Object.freeze()

b.label: Record & Tuple

slides.title: B wins. Deep immutability by default.

verdicts.title: Shallow freeze only
verdicts.text: Object.freeze doesn't freeze nested objects. You need deepFreeze or a library. And frozen objects still compare by reference.

verdicts.title: Deeply immutable + value equality
verdicts.text: Records (#{ }) and Tuples (#[ ]) are immutable all the way down. And they compare by VALUE — #{a:1} === #{a:1} is true.

slides.takeaway: Record and Tuple solve immutability AND equality in one feature.

slides.title: The killer feature: value equality.
slides.sub: Two identical records are equal. No more deep-equal libraries.

items.text: Works as Map keys and in Sets

slides.teaser: Next: Claude Code + MCP — give AI access to your entire stack
